channel.title: Foreign Tourists on Bali Kini
channel.description: Recent content in Foreign Tourists on Bali Kini

item.title: Bali Tourism Earns Rp176 Trillion in 2025, Economy Grows 5.58% in Q1 2026
item.description:
Bali has once again reaffirmed its position as the backbone of Indonesia’s tourism industry. Governor Wayan Koster revealed that Bali’s tourism sector generated Rp176 trillion in foreign exchange in 2025 — equivalent to 55 percent of Indonesia’s total national tourism foreign exchange of Rp320 trillion.

Koster’s statement, backed by data from the Central Statistics Agency (BPS), is a testament to the Island of the Gods’ outsized contribution to the Indonesian economy.

item.title: Bali's Hotel & Restaurant Tax Hits Rp2.9 Trillion Despite 7% Tourist Drop
item.description:
Despite a notable decline in foreign tourist arrivals, Bali’s tourism revenue is painting a surprisingly optimistic picture. The province’s Hotel and Restaurant Tax (PHR) through the end of May 2026 reached Rp2.9 trillion — a jump of approximately Rp300 billion compared to the same period in 2025, when the figure stood at Rp2.62 trillion.

PHR Figures: January–May 2026

Bali Governor Wayan Koster revealed the data at several tourism forums in late May and early June 2026. The official provincial figures show:
